step.title: Transition and Handoff
step.goal: Re-evaluate the verdict after fix application. Present the draft→ready gate. On user confirm, transition status; on decline, leave at draft.

substep.title: Re-Tally Findings
Append Verify Log to Worklog
Assert Ticket Is Deliverable-Only
substep.action: Before presenting the gate, confirm the ticket body contains NO process preamble or phase summaries — no "Status:" line, and none of the "Q↔R Readiness Summary / Design Summary / Structure Outline / Writing Plan" headings (those live in the worklog). If any are present, surface as a finding and strip them before the draft → ready flip — the ticket ships as deliverable-only.

substep.title: Present draft → ready Gate

check.action:
Edit the ticket frontmatter: change status from "draft" to "ready".
Stamp last_updated: uv run {base_dir}/scripts/stamp-frontmatter.py --file "{{ticket_path}}" --note "V-phase complete; draft → ready. Verdict: {{final_verdict}}. Findings: {{total}} ({{resolved}} resolved, {{waived}} waived)."
Re-validate. Halt if validation fails.
Keep status: draft. Stamp last_updated with note: "V-phase complete; verdict {{final_verdict}}; status kept at draft per user choice. {{open_concerns_summary}}."
Append a note to the worklog: "Verify-phase loop-back at gate: {{user_specified_target}}." Stamp last_updated. Exit skill. User invokes target phase via menu; re-runs ticket-verify after.

substep.title: Present Final Handoff

section.title: The draft → ready gate is the last firm checkpoint
section.description:
Spec §10.3: "After approved fixes are applied, the agent asks the user: 'Transition to ready?' User confirms → frontmatter status: ready. The ticket is now in the backlog, available for implementation-QRDSPIV pickup."
This gate is critical="true" — cannot be auto-skipped, even in YOLO mode. Status transition affects downstream backlog visibility (thoughts backlog next will surface ready tickets but not drafts). Wrong transition has real cost.

section.title: When the user stays at draft
section.description: Staying at draft is a valid choice — the user may want to iterate further (e.g., the verdict was CONDITIONAL_PASS but the user wants to resolve a Medium-waived issue before transitioning). Don't push back; record the choice in last_updated_note and exit cleanly. The user re-invokes ticket-verify after addressing concerns.

section.title: Pi runtime mapping reminder
section.description: Per QRDSPIV §Pi Runtime Mapping, the draft → ready gate maps to `request_decision` (or `show_outcome` in some flows). At the OpenCode harness layer, the Question tool serves the same role. The semantic is identical: a human-gated transition with an explicit yes/no/loop-back outcome.
